name: ephios

metadata.name: ephios
metadata.summary: ephios is a tool to manage shifts for medical services.

documentation:
![tests](https://github.com/ephios-dev/ephios/workflows/tests/badge.svg)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/ephios/badge/?version=latest)](https://docs.ephios.de/en/latest/?badge=latest)
[![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/ephios)](https://pypi.org/project/ephios/)
[![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/ephios-dev/ephios/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/ephios-dev/ephios?branch=main)
[![translated by Weblate](https://hosted.weblate.org/widgets/ephios/-/svg-badge.svg)](https://hosted.weblate.org/engage/ephios/)


# ephios

ephios is a tool to manage shifts for medical services.
Planners can create events for which volunteer help is required (e.g. security/medical services, beach patrols, exercises).
An event can contain multiple shifts for which different processes can be applied for signup 
(e.g. a direct confirmation for an event or an "application" that has to be accepted first).
The volunteers can register for the respective shifts via a clearly arranged web interface.
The planners can then assign personnel and have an overview of the current status.
Around this central feature there are further supporting functions like the management of the volunteers and their
qualifications or an overview of the volunteer hours worked. A flexible group systems helps to support complex permission
szenarios. The functionality can be extended using plugins.

## Documentation
You can find the documentation for ephios at [Read the Docs](https://docs.ephios.de/en/latest). This includes
the user guide and installation instructions.

## Contributing
Contributions to ephios are very welcome. You can find information about contributing at our [Contribution page](https://docs.ephios.de/en/latest/development/contributing.html)
We are using Weblate for translations, you can also contribute [there](https://hosted.weblate.org/engage/ephios/).

classes.name: ephios.modellogging.log.BaseLogConfig

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.log.BaseLogConfig.initial_log_recorders
documentation:
Initial log recorders are always added after model __init__.
Therefore, recorders added here should not do any db operations.
If you need those, add them manually, e.g. at the beginning of a form's save().

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.log.BaseLogConfig.related_logentries
documentation: Return a queryset with all logentries that should be shown with the instance.

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.log.BaseLogConfig.object_to_attach_logentries_to
documentation:
Return a model class and primary key for the object for which the instance's logentry should be shown.
By default, show it to the object described by the logentry itself.

Returning the model instance directly might rely on fetching that object from the database,
which can break bulk loading in some cases, so we don't do that.

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.log.BaseLogConfig.save_logentry
documentation:
Save the logentry. Overwrite this method to process the logentry
bevore saving.
pylint: disable=protected-access

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.log.ModelFieldsLogConfig.__init__
documentation:
`unlogged_fields`: Specify a list of field names so that these fields don't get logged.
Other fields get logged. Defaults to \['id'\]

`attach_to_func`: Specify a function receiving an instance and returning a tuple
of model class and object pk to attach log entries to.
Defaults to the instance's class and its own pk.

`initial_recorders_func`: Specify a function receiving an instance and returning
a list of additional log recorders.

classes.name: ephios.modellogging.middleware.LoggingRequestMiddleware
documentation:

available to the logging utilities to allow tracking of the
authenticated user making a change.

classes.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder.record
documentation:
Record changes for change action and field state for creation and deletion.
If action is CHANGE,\`\`db_instance\`\` is set to an instance freshly fetched from the db.

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder.is_changed
documentation: Returns whether the values have changed and should be included in the log.

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder.key
documentation: Some key to identify the field

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder.serialize
documentation: dump this change to a dict

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder.deserialize
documentation: load this change from a dict.

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder.change_statements
documentation: Yield human readable statements for reporting about changes.

methods.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.BaseLogRecorder.value_statements
documentation:
Yield human readable statements about the current value.
Used for reporting about created and deleted instances.

A Log Recorder is used to record changes made to a model instance that can later be included in a log entry.
A recorder follows a strict lifecycle:

> - creation at model instance initialisation or later
>
> - getting `attached` to an instance
>
> - record\`\`ing a change from an instance and the way it exists in the db, or just saving the current state
>
> - `is_changed` is called to find out whether the recorder should be included in the log
>
> - `key` is used to merge multiple log entries into one, should there be multiple `save` calls
>
> - `serialize` is called to save the state into json. The `slug` is then added to later identify the corresponding Recorder class.
>
> - `deserialize` is called when we want to display a recorder's recordings after some time.
>   deserialized recorders will never be serialized again, as e.g. a model field might not exist anymore
>
> - `change_statements` (for change action) and `value_statements` (for create and delete action)
>   : then return dicts to be fed into a statement template.

Statement templates accept a `label` value, an optional `verb`, and either an `objects` list or
a `value` with - optionally - an additional `old_value`.

classes.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.PermissionLogRecorder
documentation:
This recorder records users and groups that have object permissions for the logged instance.
If the logged model is User or Group, consider using the DerivedFieldsLogRecorder to record Permissions.

classes.name: ephios.modellogging.recorders.DerivedFieldsLogRecorder
documentation:
This recorder lets you provide a `derive` function you can use to derive fields for the log from the instance.
Return value must be a dict mapping human readable labels to values.

functions.name: ephios.extra.templatetags.utils.setvar
documentation:
as an alternative to using with blocks and template, use
`{% setvar value|filter:param as var %}` to save a value to a variable in a template.
The builtin `firstof` doesn't work, because it converts values to strings.
From here: https://pythoncircle.com/post/701/how-to-set-a-variable-in-django-template/

classes.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.add
documentation:
Add a node to the graph, with the given children.
If the node already exists, the children are added to the existing ones.
Children or parents not already in the graph are added as well.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.remove_edge
documentation: Remove an edge from the graph, throwing KeyError if it does not exist.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.descendants
documentation: Return all nodes that are reachable from the given node excluding the node itself.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.ancestors
documentation: Return all nodes that can reach the given node excluding the node itself.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.spread_from
documentation: Return all nodes that are reachable from the given nodes.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.spread_reverse
documentation: Return all nodes that can reach any of the given nodes.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph._bfs
documentation:
Return all nodes reachable starting at the given nodes.
Use `extend_with` to define what nodes to expand to.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.topological_sort
documentation:
Return a topological sort of the graph.
Throws a ValueError if the graph contains cycles.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.is_acyclic
documentation: Return True if the graph is acyclic.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.roots
documentation: Return all nodes that have no parents.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.keep_only
documentation: Remove all nodes that are not in the given set.

methods.name: ephios.extra.graphs.DirectedGraph.remove_node
documentation: Remove a node from the graph, by default keeping the edges intact.
This class implements a directed graph using adjacency collections.

functions.name: ephios.extra.permissions._make_permission_names_qualified

Return a list of permission names in the form "app_label.codename" for the given permissions codenames.
If obj is given, the app_label is inferred from the object's meta.
Otherwise, the permission name must already be qualified.

classes.name: ephios.extra.permissions.PermissionField
documentation:
This boolean field takes an additional list of permissions and a permission_target and ist true by default if the
permission_target (user or group) has all given permissions.
It requires a permission_target attribute on the form as well as calling the appropriate
methods which is taken care of by PermissionFormMixin

classes.name: ephios.extra.permissions.PermissionFormMixin
documentation: Mixin for django.forms.ModelForm that handles permission updates for all ephios.extra.permissions.PermissionField on that form

classes.name: ephios.extra.mixins.CustomPermissionRequiredMixin
documentation:
We modify Django's Mixin to support object permissions:
* Logged in users without permission get 403
* not logged in users get redirected to login

Set accept_object_perms to False to disable
object permissions (e.g. on create views).

classes.name: ephios.extra.mixins.StaffRequiredMixin
documentation: Verify that the current user is staff.

classes.name: ephios.extra.mixins.CanonicalSlugDetailMixin

methods.name: ephios.extra.mixins.CanonicalSlugDetailMixin.get_canonical_slug
documentation:
Override this method to customize what slug should be considered
canonical.
Alternatively, define the get_canonical_slug method on this view's
object class. In that case, this method will never be called.

A mixin that enforces a canonical slug in the url.
If a urlpattern takes a object's pk and slug as arguments and the slug url
argument does not equal the object's canonical slug, this mixin will
redirect to the url containing the canonical slug.

filter a shift queryset. Can't filter based on associated events, because we need

functions.name: ephios.core.signup.views.prevent_getting_participant_from_request_user
documentation:
To prevent plugin authors from accessing the participant using request.user, the SignupView
uses this method to block access to `as_participant` of the request.user attribute value.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.views.SignupView
documentation:
This View reacts to the signup or decline buttons being pressed using a POST request.
Beware that request.user might be anonymous. You should only act on participants acquired
using `get_participant`.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.slug
documentation: A unique identifier for this signup flow.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.verbose_name
documentation: The human-readable name of this signup flow.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.description
documentation: A human-readable description of this signup flow.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.uses_requested_state
documentation: Whether this signup flow uses the requested state.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.perform_signup
documentation:
Perform the signup for the given participant.
kwargs are passed from the signup view and can be used to customize the signup.
Usually it's the cleaned_data from the signup form.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.perform_decline
documentation: Perform the decline for the given participant.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.get_signup_info
documentation: Return key/value pairs about the configuration to show in exports etc.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow.get_configuration_form
documentation: Return a form to configure this signup flow.

Abstract base class for signup flow.

A signup flow defines the logic for how a participant signs up for a shift.
It checks if the participant is eligible to sign up, and if so, creates a participation object.
It is not responsible for what the participant does inside the shift, only for the process of signing up.
It provides forms for disposition and configuration.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.forms.BaseParticipationForm.get_customization_notification_info
documentation:
Return a list of human-readable messages for changed participation attributes responsibles should be informed about.
This should not include the participation state, but customization aspects such as individual times, detailed disposition information, etc.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.forms.SignupConfigurationForm
documentation: A form class to base signup flow and shift structure configuration forms on.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.slug
documentation: A unique identifier for this structure.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.verbose_name
documentation: The human-readable name of this structure.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.description
documentation: A human-readable description of this structure.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.disposition_participation_form_class
documentation:
This form will be used for participations in disposition.
Set to None if you don't want to support the default disposition.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.signup_form_class
documentation: This form will be used for participations in signup.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.get_configuration_form
documentation:
This form will be used to configure this structure.
The cleaned data will be saved to shift.structure_configuration

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.get_checkers
documentation: Return a list of checkers that should be run when validating signup actions.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.render
documentation:
Render the state/participations of the shift.
Match the signature of template.render for use with the include template tag:
{% include shift.structure %}
By default, this loads `shift_state_template_name` and renders it using context from `get_shift_state_context_data`.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.get_signup_info
documentation: Return key/value pairs about the configuration to show in exports etc.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.get_list_export_data
documentation:
Returns a list of list of dictionaries corresponding to participations or positions in this shift.
This should include all participations regardless of state or type
as well as unfilled positions.
The dictionary must contain the following items:

- "participation"
    contains a participation object
    can be None only if you provide an unfilled position
- "required_qualifications"
    contains a collection of Qualification items required for a position
    can be empty for unmatched participations
- "description":
    contains a string describing the position
    should be empty for unmatched participations

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.get_participant_count_bounds
documentation:
Return a tuple of min, max for how many participants are allowed for the shift.
Use None for any value if it is not specifiable.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.get_signup_stats
documentation: Return an instance of SignupStats for the shift.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure.has_customized_signup
documentation: Return whether the participation was customized in a way specific to this shift structure.

Abstract base class for shift structure.

A shift structure defines what a participant does inside the shift.
There may be no structure at all or some elaborate staff hierarchy.

The structure is responsible for rendering the participations of the shift.

functions.name: ephios.core.signup.disposition.addable_users
documentation:
Return queryset of user objects that can be added to the shift.
This also includes users that already have a participation, as that might have gotten removed in JS.

This also includes users that can normally not see the event. The permission will be added accordingly.
If needed, this method could be moved to signup flows.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.disposition.DispositionBaseModelFormset

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.disposition.DispositionBaseModelFormset.forms
documentation:
This formset is meant to only work with existing participation objects.
If a user submits a formset including a form for a deleted participation
(e.g. due to getting dispatched being deleted), we need to not include them.
BaseDispositionParticipationForm raises MissingParticipation if instantiated
without an instance.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.disposition.DispositionBaseModelFormset.save_existing
documentation: Existing participation state overwrites the getting dispatched state.

To allow us to dynamically add server-side rendered forms to a formset
we patch a way to change the starting index.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.base.BasicSignupFlowConfigurationForm
documentation: Configuration form with basic fields relevant for most signup flows.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.base.BaseSignupFlow

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.base.BaseSignupFlow.perform_signup
documentation:
Creates and/or configures a participation object for a given participant and sends out notifications.
Passes the participation and kwargs to configure_participation to do configuration specific to the signup flow.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.base.BaseSignupFlow.perform_decline
documentation: Create and configure a declining participation object for the given participant. `kwargs` may contain further instructions from a e.g. a form.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.base.BaseSignupFlow._configure_participation
documentation:
Configure the given participation object for signup according to the method's configuration.
You need at least to set the participations state, as that is not done with the participation form.
`kwargs` contains the signup form's cleaned_data. The participation object will be saved after this method.
Return the participation.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.base.BaseSignupFlow.get_signup_info
documentation: Return key/value pairs about the configuration to show in exports etc.
Signup flow with some base implementations for common methods.

functions.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.get_conflicting_participations
documentation:
Return a queryset of participations of a participant in conflict with
a (potential) participation specified by the arguments.

    Parameters:
        participant: AbstractParticipant to check conflict for
        shift: conflicting participations for this shift
        start_time, end_time: specify times other than the default times of `shift`
        total (bool): If True, only return conflicts that can't be
                      resolved by only participating in only part of `shift`.

    Returns:
        a queryset of participations

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupError
documentation: Superclass of errors used in the signup mechanism.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.SignupDisallowedError
documentation: Error to return if the participant cannot sign up.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.ParticipantUnfitError
documentation:
More specific error to return if the participant cannot
sign up, because they do not meet the requirements (as per shift structure).

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.DeclineDisallowedError
documentation: Error to return if the participant cannot decline a shift.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.ActionDisallowedError
documentation: Error to return if the participant cannot perform any action on a shift.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.ImproperlyConfiguredError
documentation:
Error to return if any signup action cannot be performed
for technical or configuration reasons.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupActionValidator

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupActionValidator.get_signup_errors
documentation: Return a list of errors that prevent the participant from signing up.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupActionValidator.get_decline_errors
documentation: Return a list of errors that prevent the participant from declining.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupActionValidator.get_action_errors
documentation: Return a list of errors that prevent the participant from performing any action.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupActionValidator.can_sign_up
documentation: Return whether the participant is allowed to perform signup.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupActionValidator.can_decline
documentation: Whether the participant can decline the shift.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.flow.participant_validation.BaseSignupActionValidator.can_customize_signup
documentation: Return whether the participant gets shown the option to customize their participation.

This class is initialized with a participant and a shift.
It computes whether the participant can perform certain signup actions.

classes.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.base.BaseShiftStructure

documentation: Return key/value pairs about the configuration to show in exports etc.

methods.name: ephios.core.signup.structure.base.BaseShiftStructure.get_shift_state_context_data

Shift structure with some base implementations for common methods.

functions.name: ephios.core.services.health.healthchecks.run_healthchecks
documentation: health checks are meant to monitor the health of the application while it is running
in contrast there are django checks which are meant to check the configuration of the application

methods.name: ephios.core.services.health.healthchecks.AbstractHealthCheck.slug
documentation: Return a unique slug for this health check.

methods.name: ephios.core.services.health.healthchecks.AbstractHealthCheck.name

methods.name: ephios.core.services.health.healthchecks.AbstractHealthCheck.check

functions.name: ephios.core.services.mail.cid.encoder_linelength
documentation:
RFC1341 mandates that base64 encoded data may not be longer than 76 characters per line
https://www.w3.org/Protocols/rfc1341/5_Content-Transfer-Encoding.html section 5.2

classes.name: ephios.core.services.mail.cid.CustomEmail

methods.name: ephios.core.services.mail.cid.CustomEmail._create_mime_attachment
documentation:
Convert the content, mimetype pair into a MIME attachment object.

If the mimetype is message/rfc822, content may be an
email.Message or EmailMessage object, as well as a str.
The following code is based on the pretix implementation [source]
for embedding cid images in emails.
It is released under the Apache License 2.0.
If you did not receive a copy of the license, please refer to
https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.html
source:
https://github.com/pretix/pretix/blob/a08272571b7b67a3f41e02cf05af8183e3f94a02/src/pretix/base/services/mail.py

functions.name: ephios.core.services.mail.send.prepare_plaintext
documentation:
Prepare the given plaintext for inclusion in the email.
* Replace newlines with CRLF

functions.name: ephios.core.services.mail.send.prepare_html_part
documentation:
Transform the given rendered HTML into a multipart/related MIME part.
* Inline CSS
* replace image URLs with cid: URLs

functions.name: ephios.core.services.participation.send_participation_finished
documentation: This method is registered in signals.py as a receiver of `periodic_signal`.

functions.name: ephios.core.services.qualification.top_level_set_of_qualifications
documentation:
Compute a minimum set of qualifications that include every qualification in the given set, assuming
all (transitive) inclusions from the given universe.

functions.name: ephios.core.services.qualification.essential_set_of_qualifications
documentation:
This function takes an iterable of qualifications and
returns the qualifications that are essential in the
given set while the category is meant to be shown with the user.

functions.name: ephios.core.services.qualification.collect_all_included_qualifications
documentation:
Compute the set of all qualifications that are included in the given set of qualifications, assuming
all (transitive) inclusions from the given universe.

functions.name: ephios.core.services.qualification.uuids_of_qualifications_fulfilling_any_of
documentation:
Return uuids of all qualifications fulfilling any of the given qualifications,
assuming all (transitive) inclusions from the given universe.

classes.name: ephios.core.services.qualification.QualificationUniverse
documentation:
This class stores all qualifications and their inclusions as a graph.
You can get a copy of the graph with get_graph() and use it to do computations on qualifications.
The graph is invalidated after changes to qualifications.

functions.name: ephios.core.services.matching.skill_level
documentation:
skill_level is some float in the range [0,1] that expresses
how far up in the qualification hierarchy the required qualifications are.

functions.name: ephios.core.services.matching.score_pairing
documentation:
Score the pairing of participant and  position.
Skill here means a set of qualification.

class_variables.name: ephios.core.services.matching.Position.designated_for
documentation: designated by disposition

class_variables.name: ephios.core.services.matching.Position.preferred_by
documentation: preferred by participant (less important)

class_variables.name: ephios.core.services.matching.Position.aux_score
documentation: additional score control in range [0,1]

functions.name: ephios.core.services.files.redirect_to_file_download
documentation: Shortcut for redirecting to the ticketed media file download view.

classes.name: ephios.core.services.files.UserContentView
documentation: Use this on views that serve user content/media files.

classes.name: ephios.core.services.files.FileTicketView
documentation: Deliver user content identified by a short-lived secret.

classes.name: ephios.core.services.files.EphiosMediaFileMiddleware
documentation: Ensure only media files are served from the media domain.

methods.name: ephios.core.consequences.BaseConsequenceHandler.execute
documentation: Gets a consequence and tries to execute whatever it is the consequence wants to happen.

methods.name: ephios.core.consequences.BaseConsequenceHandler.render
documentation:
Return html describing the action to be done as a consequence of what.
Return None if you cannot handle this consequence.

methods.name: ephios.core.consequences.BaseConsequenceHandler.filter_queryset
documentation:
Return a filtered that excludes consequences with the slug of this class that the user is not allowed to edit.
Consequences should also be annotated with values needed for rendering.

methods.name: ephios.core.forms.events.BasePluginFormMixin.is_function_active
documentation:
When building forms for additional features, return whether that feature is enabled for the form instance.
With the default template, if this is True, the collapse is expanded on page load.

methods.name: ephios.core.services.notifications.types.AbstractNotificationHandler.get_actions
documentation:
Return a list of (label, url) tuples that make this notification actionable.
The first url will be used as the primary action in push notifications.

methods.name: ephios.core.services.notifications.types.AbstractNotificationHandler.get_actions_with_referrer
documentation:
Annotate the actions returned by any subclass with a reference to the originating notification.
This is used by EphiosNotificationMiddleware to mark the corresponding notification as read
once the user clicks on the link.

methods.name: ephios.core.services.notifications.types.ParticipationCustomizationNotification.send
documentation: pylint: disable=arguments-differ

methods.name: ephios.core.services.notifications.types.ResponsibleConfirmedParticipationCustomizedNotification.send
documentation: pylint: disable=arguments-differ

classes.name: ephios.core.management.commands.convert_mariadb_uuids.Command
documentation: taken from https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/pull/11912/files licensed under BSD-3
Django5.0 UUID fields cause Errors with MariaDB 10.7+
This management command converts old UUID columns to the new format
see https://github.com/wagtail/wagtail/pull/11912

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.html_head
documentation:
This signal allows you to put code inside the HTML `<head>` tag
of every page. You will get the request as the keyword argument
`request` and are expected to return HTML.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.brand_logo_static_path
documentation:
This signal allows you to overwrite the default brand logo path.
You will get the request as the keyword argument
`request` and are expected to return a string containing the path to
a static file.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_consequence_handlers
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get all known consequence handlers. Receivers should return a list of
subclasses of `ephios.core.consequences.BaseConsequenceHandler`.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_shift_structures
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get all known shift structures. Receivers should return a list of
subclasses of `ephios.core.signup.structure.abstract.AbstractShiftStructure`.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_signup_flows
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get all known signup flows. Receivers should return a list of
subclasses of `ephios.core.signup.flow.abstract.AbstractSignupFlow`.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.participant_signup_checkers
documentation:
This signal is sent out so receivers can prevent signup for a shift or provide feedback for dispatchers.
Receivers are expected to return a list of functions receiving a `method` and `participant` argument
and optionally raising fitting subclasses of `ephios.core.signup.checkers.SignupActionError`.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.footer_link
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get links for that page footer. Receivers should return a dict of
with keys being the text and values being the url to link to.
Receivers will receive a `request` keyword argument.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.nav_link
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get links for the main navbar. Receivers should return a list of dicts
containing key-value-pairs for 'label', 'url' and a boolean flag 'active'. An optional key 'group' can
contain a label for a group under which the link should be displayed. The special group `NAV_USERPROFILE_KEY`
is reserved for the group under the users name.
Receivers will receive a `request` keyword argument.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.navbar_html
documentation:
This signal allows you to put additional arbitrary content inside
the navbar. You will get the request as the keyword argument
`request` and are expected to return HTML.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.settings_sections
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get sections for the settings. Receivers should return a list of dicts
containing key-value-pairs for 'label', 'url', 'group' and a boolean flag 'active'. Only views that the current user is
allowed to view should be returned. Common group choices are `SETTINGS_PERSONAL_SECTION_KEY`
and `SETTINGS_MANAGEMENT_SECTION_KEY` but can be any other value as well.
Receivers will receive a `request` keyword argument.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.participant_from_request
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get a participant from a request with an unauthenticated user.
Return a subclass of AbstractParticipant or None if you cannot provide a participant.
The first non-None return-value will be used.
Receivers will receive a `request` keyword argument.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.event_forms
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get a list of form instances to show on the event create and update views.
You receive an `Optional[event]` and `request` keyword arg you should use to create an instance of your form.
Subclass {py:class}`ephios.core.forms.events.BasePluginFormMixin` to customize the rendering behavior.
If all forms are valid, `save` will be called on your form.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.event_info
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get additional information to display in the general section of the event
detail view. Receivers will receive an `event` and `request` keyword arg to generate the information.
Receivers should return html that is added below the event description.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.shift_info
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get additional information to display in the shift box of the event
detail view. Receivers will receive a `shift` and `request` keyword arg to generate the information.
Receivers should return html that is added below the participations.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.shift_forms
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get a list of form instances to show on the shift create and update views.
You receive a `shift` and `request` keyword arg you should use to create an instance of your form.
Subclass {py:class}`ephios.core.forms.events.BasePluginFormMixin` to customize the rendering behavior.
If all forms are valid, `save` will be called on your form.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_notification_types
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get all notification types that can be sent out to a user or participant.
Receivers should return a list of subclasses of ``ephios.core.notifications.types.AbstractNotificationHandler``

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_notification_backends
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get all backends that can handle sending out notifications.
Receivers should return a list of subclasses of ``ephios.core.notifications.backends.AbstractNotificationBackend``

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_healthchecks
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get all health checks that can be run to monitor the health of the application.
Receivers should return a list of subclasses of ``ephios.core.services.health.AbstractHealthCheck``

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.periodic_signal
documentation: This signal is called periodically, at least every 15 minutes.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.participation_finished
documentation:
This signal is sent out once for every confirmed participation after the participation ended and
the `finished` flag is set to True. Changing the shift date to the future will not cause this to be called again.
Exceptions in receivers are ignored, so make sure your code is robust enough to handle errors and retry if needed.
This signal is based on `periodic_signal` and provides a `participation` keyword argument.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_event_bulk_action
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get a list of actions that a user can perform on a list of events.
Receivers should return a list of actions. Each action is represented by a dict with the keys `url`, `label` and `icon`.
Once the user wants to perform the action, a POST request will be issued to this URL. The `bulk_action` field
will contain a list of event ids on which the action should be performed.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.event_action
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get a list of actions that a user can perform on a single event. The actions are
displayed in the dropdown menu on the event detail view.
Receivers receive a `event` and `request` keyword argument.
Each action is represented by a dict with the keys `url`, `label` and `icon`.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.homepage_info
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get additional information to display on the homepage.
Receivers receive a `request` keyword argument. Receivers should return html that will be rendered inside a card.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.register_group_permission_fields
documentation:
This signal is sent out to get a list of permission fields that should be displayed on the group form.
Receivers should return a list of tuples of the form `(field_name, field)`. field must be an instance of
`ephios.extra.permissions.PermissionField`.

variables.name: ephios.core.signals.oidc_update_user
documentation:
This signal is sent out to update a user when they login with oidc. Receivers receive `user` and `claims`
keyword arguments with a user object and the claims from the oidc provider.

functions.name: ephios.core.plugins.get_all_plugins
documentation: Return the EphiosPluginMeta classes of all plugins found in the installed Django apps.

functions.name: ephios.core.plugins.get_enabled_plugins
documentation: Return a subset of all plugin meta classes - those that are enabled

functions.name: ephios.core.plugins.is_receiver_path_enabled
documentation:
Return True only if `searchpath` (e.g. 'ephios.plugins.basesignup.signals')
relies in a module that is either an enabled plugin or considered ephios core.
Uses a cache that gets reset when enabled plugins preference changes.

classes.name: ephios.core.plugins.PluginSignal
documentation: Signal that will only be send out to enabled plugins and ephios core.

classes.name: ephios.core.plugins.PluginConfig
documentation: Superclass for Plugin App Configs. Might use this in the future to implement new features.
